senses.definition: having expertise, intelligence, or skills

examples.x: She is an able linguist, fluent in multiple languages.
As an able engineer, he successfully designed and implemented the new system.
The team of able researchers uncovered groundbreaking discoveries in the field of medicine.
His able leadership during the crisis ensured the company's stability and growth.
With her able negotiation skills, she secured a favorable deal for her clients.

senses.definition: so unreasonable or illogical that it provokes disbelief or laughter

examples.x: It's absurd to think that the Earth is flat in this day and age.
The notion that cats can fly is utterly absurd.
His explanation for being late was so absurd that no one believed him.
The comedian's jokes were so absurd that they had the audience in stitches.
The idea of a pineapple pizza might sound absurd to some, but it's actually quite popular.

senses.definition: able to notice things or think quickly

examples.x: The alert security guard spotted the intruder before they could enter the building.
She remained alert during the meeting, ready to contribute valuable insights at any moment.
The alert hiker noticed the subtle changes in the weather and decided to head back before the storm hit.
The detective's alert mind quickly pieced together the clues to solve the mystery.

senses.definition: possessing great skill in a certain field

examples.x: She is an accomplished pianist, having performed at prestigious concert halls around the world.
As an accomplished writer, he has published numerous bestselling novels.
The accomplished chef received a Michelin star for her culinary expertise.
He is an accomplished surgeon, renowned for his precision and skill in the operating room.
The accomplished artist's paintings are displayed in galleries across the globe.

senses.definition: (of a person) able to express oneself clearly and effectively

examples.x: She's articulate, conveying her thoughts and ideas with clarity and confidence.
The articulate writer conveys emotions and experiences through vivid descriptions and storytelling.
The articulate teacher explains concepts in a way that students easily understand.
The articulate politician effectively communicates their policies and vision to the public.
The professor is articulate, always able to convey difficult concepts in a coherent way.

senses.definition: very smart

examples.x: The brainy scientist made significant breakthroughs in the field of quantum physics.
His brainy approach to problem-solving earned him a reputation as a genius among his peers.
The brainy student breezed through the challenging math exam with ease.
The brainy detective quickly pieced together the clues to solve the mysterious case.
Despite his young age, he's an incredibly brainy child, already showing signs of exceptional intelligence.

senses.definition: extremely violent and cruel

examples.x: The brutal attack left him with severe injuries and trauma.
The dictator's regime was known for its brutal suppression of dissent.
The crime scene was a testament to the brutal nature of the crime.
She couldn't bring herself to watch the news coverage of the brutal murder.
The documentary exposed the brutal living conditions endured by factory workers.
The soldiers faced a brutal battle with no hope of surrender.

senses.definition: possessing the needed skills or knowledge to do something well

examples.x: As a competent programmer, he was able to troubleshoot and fix the software bug quickly.
The company only hires competent employees who demonstrate proficiency in their respective fields.
His competent handling of the crisis earned him praise from both colleagues and superiors.
She proved herself to be a competent leader by effectively managing the team through the project.
The pilot's competent navigation skills enabled a smooth and safe flight despite adverse weather conditions.

senses.definition: (of a person) ready to argue and often arguing

examples.x: His argumentative nature often leads to heated discussions with his peers.
The couple's argumentative relationship made family gatherings tense and uncomfortable.
Her argumentative demeanor made it difficult for others to work with her collaboratively.
The argumentative tone of the meeting hindered productive discussion and problem-solving.
Despite his argumentative tendencies, he was respected for his critical thinking skills.

senses.definition: easily annoyed and quick to anger

examples.x: He was known for his bad-tempered demeanor, often snapping at those around him.
The bad-tempered customer stormed out of the store after arguing with the cashier.
Dealing with his bad-tempered landlord was one of the downsides of renting the apartment.
The bad-tempered boss made the workplace environment hostile and unpleasant.
The bad-tempered cat hissed and scratched whenever anyone approached it.

senses.definition: showing impolite behavior in a manner that is amusing or endearing

examples.x: The cheeky child giggled mischievously as he played pranks on his siblings.
His cheeky grin and witty remarks always lightened the mood.
The comedian's cheeky jokes had the audience roaring with laughter.
She made a cheeky comment about his fashion sense, teasing him in a friendly way.
His cheeky remarks often landed him in trouble with his teachers.

senses.definition: doing things or moving in a way that lacks control and care, usually causing accidents

examples.x: He's known for his clumsy nature, always tripping over his own feet.
The clumsy waiter spilled soup on the customer's lap.
Her clumsy attempts at dancing drew laughter from the crowd.
The new employee's clumsy handling of fragile items resulted in several broken dishes.
She felt embarrassed by her clumsy stumble in front of her classmates.

senses.definition: taking excessive pride in oneself

examples.x: His conceited attitude made it difficult for others to warm up to him.
The conceited model couldn't stop admiring herself in the mirror.
Her conceited behavior turned people away, leaving her with few friends.
Despite his conceited demeanor, deep down, he struggled with feelings of insecurity.
Her conceited remarks about her appearance grated on her friends' nerves.

senses.definition: a person who is not brave to do things that other people find unchallenging

examples.x: The coward ran away from the battle, leaving his comrades to face the enemy alone.
Don't be a coward; stand up for what you believe in.
The soldier was labeled a coward for deserting his post during the war.
Nobody respects a coward who avoids difficult situations.
His reputation suffered when he was branded a coward after backing down from a confrontation.

senses.definition: slightly strange in behavior, appearance, or ideas

examples.x: Her eccentric fashion choices always turn heads.
The artist was known for his eccentric approach to painting.
People found his eccentric ideas both interesting and confusing.
The eccentric billionaire spent his fortune on bizarre inventions.
The eccentric professor often held class in the park.

senses.definition: cruel and unkind toward others

examples.x: The teacher's harsh criticism demoralized the students.
The harsh punishment was disproportionate to the offense committed.
His harsh tone during the meeting alienated his colleagues.
She regretted her harsh words during the argument, realizing they had crossed a line.
The harsh manner in which she addressed her employees created a toxic work environment.

senses.definition: well-known for a bad quality or deed

examples.x: The criminal was infamous for his string of bank robberies across the country.
The dictator's reign was infamous for its brutality and oppression.
The hotel gained an infamous reputation after a series of scandals involving its management.
The company became infamous for its unethical business practices.
The politician's infamous speech sparked outrage and controversy nationwide.

senses.definition: not open to accept beliefs, opinions, or lifestyles that are unlike one's own

examples.x: His intolerant attitude toward people of different religions created tension in the community.
She found it difficult to be friends with someone who was so intolerant of diverse viewpoints.
The intolerant remarks made by the politician sparked outrage among the public.
Intolerant individuals often find it challenging to coexist in a diverse environment.
The leader's intolerant stance on immigration led to division within the political party.

senses.definition: not caring about other people's feelings

examples.x: His insensitive remarks about her appearance left her feeling hurt and self-conscious.
She regretted her insensitive comment as soon as it left her mouth.
His insensitive behavior toward his classmates made him unpopular at school.
The politician's insensitive remarks about poverty sparked widespread outrage.
Her insensitive actions toward her friend strained their relationship.

senses.definition: tending to criticize or form negative opinions about others without considering their perspective or circumstances

examples.x: She felt uncomfortable around her judgmental relatives who always criticized her life choices.
His judgmental attitude made it difficult for him to connect with new people.
The online community can be quite judgmental, often jumping to conclusions without knowing the full story.
Her judgmental comments about his appearance made him feel self-conscious.
Growing up in a judgmental environment affected her self-esteem and confidence.
The teacher's judgmental tone discouraged the student from speaking up.

senses.definition: not open to new ideas, opinions, etc.

examples.x: His narrow-minded views on politics made it difficult to have meaningful discussions with him.
The company's narrow-minded approach to innovation stifled creativity among its employees.
Growing up in a narrow-minded community, she often felt like she didn't belong.
The politician's narrow-minded policies failed to address the needs of the diverse population.
Her narrow-minded parents disapproved of her unconventional career choice.

senses.definition: confident in expressing one's opinions, ideas, or needs in a clear, direct, and respectful manner

examples.x: She made an assertive argument during the meeting, clearly outlining her proposal.
Assertive communication is key in negotiations to ensure your needs are met without being aggressive.
The assertive tone of her voice conveyed confidence and authority.
He admired her assertive personality, which made her stand out in a competitive environment.
Assertive leaders inspire trust and motivate their teams to achieve goals.

senses.definition: giving much attention to something or someone with interest

examples.x: The attentive student always sits at the front of the class, eager to learn.
She appreciated his attentive listening during their conversation, feeling valued and understood.
The teacher praised the students for their attentive behavior during the lecture.
As an attentive parent, she always makes time to listen to her children's concerns.
His attentive gaze never wavered from the speaker, absorbing every word.

senses.definition: (of a person) careful to avoid danger or mistakes

examples.x: She walked with cautious steps along the icy sidewalk, mindful of the slippery surface.
The hikers were cautious as they navigated through the dense forest, watching out for wild animals.
The company took a cautious approach to expansion, thoroughly researching each potential market.
She gave him a cautious smile, unsure of his intentions.
The detective proceeded with cautious optimism, hoping to uncover new leads in the case.

senses.definition: expressing love and care

examples.x: The couple shared an affectionate embrace after being apart for weeks.
Their family was known for their affectionate gestures, always hugging and kissing each other.
She appreciated his affectionate nature, feeling loved and cherished in his presence.
The elderly couple held hands as they walked down the street, their affectionate bond evident to all who passed by.
They exchanged affectionate glances across the room, their love for each other evident in their eyes.

senses.definition: kind and generous toward the less fortunate

examples.x: The billionaire philanthropist was known for his charitable donations to various humanitarian causes.
The community rallied together to organize a charitable fundraiser for the victims of the natural disaster.
His charitable nature was evident in the way he always went out of his way to help those in need.
The company's charitable initiatives included sponsoring educational programs for underprivileged children.
The charitable organization provided food and shelter to homeless individuals during the harsh winter months.

senses.definition: showing kindness and understanding toward others, especially during times of difficulty or suffering

examples.x: The nurse showed compassionate care and empathy towards the elderly patients in the nursing home.
His compassionate nature led him to volunteer at the local animal shelter, where he provided comfort to abandoned pets.
In times of crisis, it's essential to have compassionate leaders who understand and address the needs of the community.
The compassionate response from volunteers and donors helped provide relief to families affected by the natural disaster.
Her compassionate gestures, such as offering a listening ear and a shoulder to cry on, provided solace to her friends in distress.

senses.definition: thoughtful of others and their feelings

examples.x: In a considerate act of kindness, the student shared his notes with a classmate who had missed a lecture due to illness.

senses.definition: expressing no fear when faced with danger or difficulty

examples.x: The courageous firefighter rushed into the burning building to rescue the trapped residents, displaying extraordinary bravery.
Maria's courageous decision to speak up against injustice inspired others to join the movement for change.
The young explorer exhibited a courageous spirit as he ventured into the uncharted wilderness in search of new discoveries.
The courageous act of confronting her fears enabled Emily to overcome her phobia of public speaking.
The rescue dog demonstrated a courageous effort in saving lives during the disaster response mission.

senses.definition: displaying calmness and seriousness in a manner that deserves respect

examples.x: Despite the difficult circumstances, the diplomat maintained a dignified composure during the peace negotiations.
Despite her illness, she faced each day with a dignified resolve, refusing to let adversity define her.
The ceremony was conducted with a dignified solemnity befitting the occasion of honoring fallen heroes.
The judge's dignified demeanor in the courtroom instilled confidence in the fairness of the legal proceedings.
In her final moments, she maintained a dignified dignity, surrounded by loved ones and at peace with herself.

senses.definition: staying loyal and dedicated to a certain person, idea, group, etc.

examples.x: Despite the challenges, she remained a faithful friend, always ready to offer support and encouragement.
The faithful employee consistently showed up to work on time and completed tasks with dedication and diligence.
Even in adversity, she remained faithful to her dreams, working tirelessly to achieve her goals.
Throughout the years, he remained faithful to his principles, never compromising his beliefs.
The faithful fans of the band waited eagerly for their latest album, demonstrating unwavering support for their music.

senses.definition: direct and honest in expressing oneself, even if some people might find it unpleasant

examples.x: Shiv appreciated Tom's frank feedback on her presentation, even though it was a bit blunt.
The boss's frank discussion about the company's financial struggles left the employees feeling uneasy but informed.
His frank admission of guilt surprised everyone in the courtroom, as they had expected him to deny the accusations.
The author's writing style was known for its frank portrayal of human emotions, often delving into uncomfortable truths.
Jenny's frank demeanor sometimes rubbed people the wrong way, but her friends valued her honesty.

senses.definition: displaying great bravery as that of a hero or heroine

examples.x: The firefighter's heroic actions saved several lives during the blazing inferno.
Despite being outnumbered, the soldiers displayed heroic valor in defending their position.
The protagonist's heroic journey to save the kingdom captivated readers of all ages.
In a moment of crisis, her heroic instincts kicked in, and she sprang into action without hesitation.
The marathon runner's heroic effort to finish the race despite a leg injury inspired spectators along the route.

senses.definition: treating guests or visitors with friendliness, warmth, and generosity

examples.x: The hostess extended a hospitable welcome to all her guests, ensuring they felt comfortable and valued.
The villagers were known for their hospitable nature, always ready to share their food and shelter with strangers in need.
The locals greeted us with hospitable smiles as we explored their quaint village nestled in the countryside.
The restaurant prides itself on its hospitable atmosphere, where diners are treated like cherished friends rather than customers.
During our vacation, we experienced the hospitable culture of the region firsthand, encountering kindness at every turn.

senses.definition: cheerful and happy

examples.x: The children's laughter filled the room as they played games and sang songs, creating a jolly atmosphere.
Despite the rainy weather, the jolly group of friends decided to go for a hike and make the best of their day.
The jolly tavern was filled with merry patrons enjoying good food, lively music, and laughter.
The jolly family gathered around the fireplace, sharing stories and laughter as they celebrated the festive season together.

senses.definition: attempting to deceive other people

examples.x: The politician's duplicitous behavior was exposed when his private emails contradicted his public statements.
The company's CEO was known for his duplicitous tactics in negotiations, often making promises he had no intention of keeping.
After discovering her partner's duplicitous nature, Emily decided to end their relationship and move on with her life.
The movie's plot revolves around the duplicitous schemes of a cunning villain who will stop at nothing to achieve his goals.
The duplicitous nature of the spy's double life made it difficult for anyone to trust him, even his closest allies.

senses.definition: acting with fairness, ensuring what is morally right and reasonable

examples.x: The company's policy of equal pay for equal work is a just practice that promotes fairness and equality in the workplace.
The teacher's disciplinary actions were based on just principles, ensuring that all students were treated fairly and with respect.
The protesters demanded a just distribution of resources and opportunities in society, advocating for fairness and social justice.
In his role as a leader, he strived to create a just society where everyone had equal access to opportunities and resources.

senses.definition: not welcoming or willing to do something because it is undesirable

examples.x: Despite his love for adventure, he was reluctant to try bungee jumping because of his fear of heights.
The students were reluctant to speak up in class, fearing ridicule from their peers.
The company was reluctant to invest in new technology due to concerns about cost and implementation.
Despite her talent, she was reluctant to perform in front of an audience because of stage fright.
The dog was reluctant to enter the water, hesitating at the edge of the pool.

senses.definition: a dishonest person with the tendency to deceive people for personal gain

examples.x: Watch out for him, he's a snake who can't be trusted.
I can't believe she would do that to her friends, what a snake!
He acted like my friend, but behind my back, he was a snake spreading rumors.
Don't be fooled by his charm, he's a snake in disguise.
She realized too late that her business partner was a snake, coiling around her trust with false promises and secret schemes to undermine her success.

senses.definition: someone who keeps dropping things

examples.x: She laughed at her friend's nickname "butterfingers" after he dropped his phone for the third time that day.
As a child, he earned the nickname "butterfingers" for his tendency to drop things.
The soccer player's teammates affectionately called him "butterfingers" after he missed an easy catch during the game.
Despite his best efforts, he couldn't shake off the reputation of being a "butterfingers" in the kitchen.
She teased her brother about his "butterfingers" whenever he struggled to catch a ball.

senses.definition: a person who is hateful or detested because of their dishonest actions

examples.x: Some dirtbags tried to blow up the restaurant with a bomb.
I can't believe he cheated on his partner; what a dirtbag.
She's such a dirtbag for spreading rumors about her coworkers.
Don't trust him; he's a total dirtbag who will stab you in the back.
He's earned his reputation as a dirtbag by constantly lying and manipulating others.

senses.definition: a person who habitually takes advantage of others' generosity without offering anything in return

examples.x: She's tired of being taken advantage of by that freeloader who never contributes anything.
He's always crashing on our couch and eating our food without ever offering to pay for anything—he's such a freeloader.
I thought he was a friend, but he turned out to be nothing more than a freeloader, always looking for a handout.
We need to address the issue of the freeloader in our group who never chips in for group expenses.
Despite contributing nothing to the household expenses, he always managed to be the first in line for dinner, earning himself the title of the family freeloader.

senses.definition: someone who behaves in an evil or immoral way

examples.x: The wretch stole money from the charity fund meant for the homeless.
He's a wretch who takes pleasure in spreading malicious rumors about others.
She's a wretch who cheats on her exams and lies to cover it up.
She's a wretch who schemes and plots to destroy the lives of those who oppose her.
He's a wretch who enjoys inflicting pain on others both physically and emotionally.

senses.definition: not willing to follow rules or instructions

examples.x: Despite repeated warnings, the unruly children disrupted the class with constant chatter and refusal to listen.
Removal from the party was inevitable due to the unruly behavior of the guests.
Blocking the streets and refusing to move until their demands were met, the unruly protesters caused traffic gridlock.
Known for his unruly temper, he often found himself in hot water with his colleagues.
Prompted by the unruly behavior of the students, the teacher called for stricter discipline measures to be implemented.

senses.definition: talking or behaving in a way that is rude, disrespectful, or too confident
someone who is deceitful, sneaky, or untrustworthy, often characterized by their ability to manipulate situations or information for personal gain

examples.x: Don't trust him; he's a sneaky weasel who will stab you in the back as soon as it benefits him.
She's always finding ways to manipulate people's opinions; she's a real weasel.
It's hard to believe anything that comes out of his mouth; he's such a weasel.
Watch out for that weasel; he'll try to twist your words to make himself look better.
You can't rely on a weasel like him to keep his promises; he's only in it for himself.
